pattern: Each maximal run of one key decomposes independently, and within a run a bounded-step counting recurrence (Tribonacci-like for the four-letter keys) tallies the groupings modulo a large prime.
hints: Different digits never combine into one letter, so split the input into maximal runs of the same digit and multiply their counts.
Within a run of length L of a key offering g letters (g is 3 for most, 4 for the keys '7' and '9'), the number of decodings depends only on L and g.
Define f(L) = f(L-1) + f(L-2) + f(L-3) (plus f(L-4) when g is 4), with f(0)=1, taking every addition modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: long run of a three-letter key

stress.description: long random sequence of digits 2-9 with frequent repeats forcing the modulo
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    out = []
    while len(out) < n:
        d = str(rng.randint(2, 9))
        run = rng.randint(1, 8)
        out.extend([d] * run)
    return [''.join(out[:n])]
stress.note: runs up to length 8 exercise both recurrence depths
